steps.title: State the no-solution condition
steps.content:
Rearrange $5x + 3 = rx + 7$ by collecting $x$ terms:
$$5x - rx = 7 - 3 \implies (5 - r)x = 4$$
For no solution, the coefficient of $x$ must equal zero (producing the false statement $0 = 4$), so $5 - r = 0$, giving $r = 5$.

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $r = 21$: $(5-21)x = 4 \Rightarrow -16x = 4 \Rightarrow x = -\frac{1}{4}$. Exactly one solution. ✗
  • Choice B — $r = 8$: $(5-8)x = 4 \Rightarrow -3x = 4 \Rightarrow x = -\frac{4}{3}$. Exactly one solution. ✗
  • Choice C — $r = 7$: $(5-7)x = 4 \Rightarrow -2x = 4 \Rightarrow x = -2$. Exactly one solution. ✗
  • Choice D — $r = 5$: $(5-5)x = 4 \Rightarrow 0 = 4$. Contradiction — no solution exists. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
